title: Werauhia balanophora
intro: Werauhia balanophora, also known as the "Queen's Tears," is a rare and endangered bromeliad species native to the tropical rainforests of Costa Rica and Panama. The plant is prized for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia balanophora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited to folk medicine due to its rarity and the lack of extensive scientific research.
taxonomy: Werauhia balanophora is a species within the plant family Bromeliaceae. This family comprises approximately 3,000 species of tropical plants, many of which are epiphytes. The Bromeliaceae family is further divided into 56 genera, with Werauhia being one of them. Werauhia balanophora is also classified as a type of air plant, utilizing its specialized leaves to absorb moisture and nutrients from the air. It is native to Central and South America.
